exploration_log.aha_moment: The recursive `parseForValidate(String,...)` and `validate(String,...)` methods follow the dependents graph. A standard DFS with a visited+inProgress set detects cycles before any recursion starts, preventing the StackOverflowError.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could pass `path` as a `Deque` rather than a `List` for cleaner add/remove, but List works fine.

alternatives: Add visited set inline inside parseForValidate()
Detect cycle during define() when dependents are added
key_decisions.decision: Pre-scan in validateAll() via checkForDependentCycles() before processing
key_decisions.reasoning: Keeps the existing recursive helpers unchanged; throws ConfigException early with clear message before any parsing occurs.

search_sequence.action: Read validateAll(), parseForValidate(), and validate() methods in ConfigDef.java (lines 566-692)
search_sequence.reasoning: Understand the existing recursive flow and where to inject cycle detection
search_sequence.result: Found that validateAll() calls parseForValidate() then validate(), both of which recurse through key.dependents without any visited tracking.

queries.query_text: Add cycle detection to ConfigDef.validateAll() using visited set, throw ConfigException with cycle path for circular dependent config chains

solve_notes: Added `checkForDependentCycles()` called from `validateAll()` that performs a DFS over the dependents graph using a `visited` and `inProgress` set. When a back-edge is detected (cycle), throws `ConfigException` listing the cycle path. Added `detectCycle()` as the recursive DFS helper.
task_text: Fix the `ConfigDef` framework in `common/config/ConfigDef.java` to detect cycles. When config A lists B as a dependent and B lists A as a dependent, calling `validate(props)` causes infinite recursion ending in `StackOverflowError`. Add cycle detection in `validateAll()` using a `Set<String>` of in-progress keys, throwing `ConfigException` with a descriptive message (listing the cycle path) when a cycle is detected.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: parseForValidate
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: seeing how dependents are recursively iterated prevents writing the cycle check incorrectly (e.g., inside the recursion instead of as a pre-scan)

thrash_preventing_defs.name: validate
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: same recursive pattern - an AI agent would check both recursive methods before deciding where to add cycle detection

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set includes only `validateAll` (edited) and the primary `parseForValidate` (the most obvious source of recursion). The thrash-preventing set additionally includes the private `validate(String,...)` method because an AI agent, seeing two recursive helpers in `validateAll`, would search for the second one to confirm both follow the same pattern before deciding on the pre-scan approach.
